result.text:
After arriving to the NATO ministerial in Helsingborg, I had a substantial meeting with @SecGenNATO Mark Rutte.

I informed the Secretary General about Russia’s increased threats to Ukraine and wider Europe from the direction of Belarus.

We need strong collective deterrence to prevent regimes in Moscow and Minsk from making mistakes.

We also discussed the latest battlefield dynamics, Ukraine’s highly successful long-range sanctions against Russia, and the need to further develop the PURL initiative.

We also spoke about peace efforts and Europe’s role. To advance diplomacy, pressure on Moscow remains critical. I reiterated that there should be no easing of sanctions.

I thanked the Secretary General for his remarkable work in strengthening NATO and his firm support for Ukraine.

Among the many things that I do not miss about serving in public office is the daily barrage of hit pieces from leftist media organizations.

My efforts towards securing our elections has unwittingly tossed me back into that war yet again.  When I was told a reporter by the name of Khaya at Talking Points Memo was interested in doing a story on why the DoJ was looking into Wayne County, MI 2024 election records, I took the call.  

During the call, I provided the reporter with the following information in support of DoJ inquiry:

-Evidence of 125,428 illegal votes being cast in 2024 election before being stopped by election integrity watchdogs in MI (i.e. #MeddlingKids)
-Evidence of MI SoS Jocelyn "we will come for you" Benson issuing unlawful election directives affirmed by 8 different courts
-Evidence that MI SoS Jocelyn "we will come for you" Benson only follows the laws she agrees with and if you don't like that she encourages you to sue her so that her "friends" that she provided $82,500 in "donations" to in the MI Supreme Court stop the suit in its tracks

She didn't print any of that...although she did share my assertion that the 2020 election was never lawfully certified (of course it was against the backdrop of characterizing me as "an election conspiracy theorist").

Why didn't she print any of the information in support of the DoJ investigation into the 2024 election in Wayne County?  She wasn't really interested in that information.  It was clear from my interview that all she wanted to know was who I was in contact with at the DoJ ("network"). I didn't share that information.

Why does she want to know who we are speaking with at the DoJ?   Progressive activists such as Khaya want the names of federal investigators so that they can advise their minions to make the lives of honest investigators a living hell and obstruct their efforts. [Democracy Defense actually conducts training for federal workers on how to obstruct honest and impartial government officials from doing their job...aka sedition]

So, if you would like to read a prime example of what I refer to as "Mad Lib" journalism (where the story is already written before they talk to you and all they want is a couple of quotes to insert), here you go.

https://t.co/U1T7EVtv2y

result.text:
For 30 years, Russia pushed the narrative that Ukraine’s aspiration to join the EU would reduce it to Europe’s raw-material appendage and degrade the country.

History has turned the story upside down.

Ukraine is becoming a backbone of high-tech security solutions for Europe and beyond. Russia is sliding into the role it once assigned to us: a raw-material appendage to China.

I suspect Putin understands the indignity of this reversal. But his obsession with Ukraine is now stronger than his instinct for national pride.

His visit to China only confirmed it. Strip away the rhetoric, and the main project is gas supplies — a symbol of Russia’s broader drift into feeding China’s economy with energy and raw materials.

Putin wants to sell. Xi is in no rush. He wants the best terms from a weakened partner.

China does not embrace Putin. It extracts value from him.

The good news is that Xi needs Putin weak. The bad news is that he still needs him.

data.translation:
Most of my life, I’ve worn a military uniform. But the vyshyvanka for me is about memory. About home, family, childhood, about the people who are no longer with us, but who are always in our thoughts.

Today is World Vyshyvanka Day. Once it was an initiative of a few students, and now Vyshyvanka Day has truly become global. Ukrainian vyshyvankas are worn in different countries, on the front lines and in the rear, in big cities and small villages.

In this, there is probably something very important for all of us – even in the most difficult times, to remember who we are and where we come from.

And I believe that the day will come when we can take off our military uniforms and once again put on embroidered shirts – in a peaceful, free, and safe Ukraine.

Happy Vyshyvanka Day! Let’s cherish what’s ours!

Eleven Ukrainian families who lost their homes due to Russia’s war received new apartments on Wednesday in a housing giveaway organized by KADORR Group and its chairman, Ruslan Kivan.

This was the second apartment transfer; during the first, KADORR Group gifted apartments to eight families.

The ceremony took place in Odesa at the company’s business center, where families were presented with apartment certificates as part of a broader effort to support internally displaced people (IDPs).

According to the company, the apartments were allocated through a draw among subscribers of the official Telegram channel of the NGO “IDPs of Ukraine,” which now has more than 97,000 followers. The organization works with Ukrainians displaced by Russia’s full-scale invasion.

result.text:
Today, I held a substantive meeting with leading representatives of the business community of Southern Sweden. 

The event was attended by 15 top managers and owners representing around 20 major enterprises and business structures across various sectors of the Swedish economy.

I thanked Swedish businesses who work in Ukraine — and stay with us even during wartime — for their principles, social mission, and resilience. 

We spoke about ways to facilitate business cooperation, including the possibility of easing travel restrictions both on national and EU level.

I also encouraged Swedish businesses to participate actively in the recovery of Ukraine, attend the upcoming Recovery Conference in Gdansk, and engage the Ukrainian community. 

Among the participants were representatives from industry, engineering, and manufacturing; energy (including renewables); infrastructure and construction; pharmaceuticals and life sciences; financial services; investments; and security technologies. 

Notable companies included: Danir Group, Sigma, Saab, Nederman Holding AB, Lindab AB, Yaskawa Nordic AB, Alfa Laval, AFRY, Hill Ceramic, Vindkraft Group, Øresundsbron, Bona AB, PEAB Group, AstraZeneca, SEB, Zhoda Investments, Tetra Pak Lund, and Axis.

I thank Professor Johan Eklund, CEO and President of the Chamber of Commerce and Industry of Southern Sweden, who chaired the meeting.

result.text:
This New York Times piece is worth your time. Here’s what is happening, as simply as I can put it.

Back in January, Trump sued the IRS, an agency he controls, demanding $10 billion over the leak of his tax returns a number of years ago.

IRS lawyers did their jobs. They wrote a memo laying out the defenses that could beat the suit, including the fact that Trump filed too late. His own lawyer was in court when the leaker pleaded guilty in October 2023, more than two years before Trump sued.

The Justice Department never showed up to court. Never argued back. Never used the defenses sitting on their desk.

The judge got suspicious and ordered both sides to explain whether they were actually opposing each other or just colluding. The day before that brief was due, Trump dropped the suit. 

Same day, his Justice Department announced a $1.776 billion taxpayer-funded “anti-weaponization fund.” 

Trump gets a formal apology. The IRS agrees to drop any audits of him and his family, even though a 2024 Times report found a loss in an ongoing audit could cost him over $100 million.

The acting Attorney General, Trump’s former criminal defense attorney, picks the five commissioners who decide who gets paid. Trump can fire any of them. Proud Boys and Oath Keepers are not ruled out.

This is the most corrupt thing I’ve ever seen from an American president.

Where in the hell are my Republican colleagues?
